LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GOODFELLOW TOP GRADE CONSTRUCTION, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT EXIST AS TO WHETHER GOODFELLOW'S FAILURES TO COMPLY WITH LABOR CODE 226 WERE "KNOWING AND INTENTIONAL" RATHER THAN "ISOLATED AND UNINTENTIONAL." (SEE KAO V. HOLIDAY (2017) 12 CAL.APP.4TH 947, 961-62.) THIS IS PARTICULARLY SO AS THE FAILURES APPARENTLY OCCURRED SOME 1,500 TIMES OVER A PERIOD OF MANY MONTHS. (PMF 5-8.) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/RU

RULING - The matter is not contested. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)
The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF OLATUNJI RAHEEM's Renewed, Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement.

Plaintiff Olatunji O. Raheem's renewed motion for preliminary approval of class action settlement is approved, subject to the following: In its motion for an award of attorneys' fees and costs to be heard at the time of final approval, class counsel shall submit declarations of counsel and/or other evidence sufficient to show counsel's lodestar for purposes of the Court conducting a cross-check of the reasonableness of the requested percentage-based fee. (See Laffitte v. Robert Half International, Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480, 503-506.)

In addition, Counsel on the phone is instructed to get in touch with Plaintiff's Counsel, look at deadlines set and agree on a Final Approval Hearing date and submit a follow up order to the Court.***

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

***After the hearing, the Court received an email that both parties have agreed to November 19, 2019 as the hearing date for Plaintiff's Motion for Final Approval and Motion for Attorney's Fees, Costs and Service Payment. ***

Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF OLATUNJI RAHEEM's Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval.

Plaintiff Olatunji O. Raheem's motion for preliminary approval of class action settlement is denied without prejudice. In order to determine whether a proposed class action settlement is fair and reasonable, a trial court "should consider relevant factors, such as the strength of plaintiffs' case, the risk, expense, complexity and likely duration of further litigation, the risk of maintaining class action status through trial, the amount offered in settlement, the extent of discovery completed and the stage of the proceedings, the experience and views of counsel, the presence of a governmental participant, and the reaction of the class members to the proposed settlement." (Dunk v. Ford Motor Co. 48 Cal.App.4th 1794, 1801.) "'The most important factor is the strength of the case for plaintiffs on the merits, balanced against the amount offered in settlement.'" (Kullar v. Foot Locker Retail, Inc. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 116, 130.) While a presumption of fairness exists if certain factors are present, "an informed evaluation cannot be made without an understanding of the amount that is in controversy and the realistic range of outcomes of the litigation." (Id. at 120.)

Here, as in Kullar, "nothing was presented to the court-no discovery, no declarations, no time records, no payroll data, nothing [citations]-to allow the court to evaluate the claim." (Munoz v. BCI Coca-Cola Bottling Co. of Los Angeles 186 Cal.App.4th 399, 409.) Plaintiff has not presented sufficient information to enable the court to evaluate the reasonableness of the proposed settlement, such as the damages that each class member sustained, the total damages the class could expect to recover if it prevails on its claim, or any explanation of the factors that were considering in discounting the potential recovery for purposes of settlement. (See Kullar, 168 Cal.App.4th at 128-131.) "While the court need not determine the ultimate merit of a claim, it is obliged to determine, at a minimum, whether a legitimate controversy exists on a legal point, so that it has some basis for assessing whether the parties' evaluation of the case is within the 'ballpark' of reasonableness." (Clark v. American Residential Services LLC (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 785, 790.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. John Briscoe of Mayall Hurley, Tel. No. 1-209-477-3833 ext. 4238 specially
appearing for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Sarah Lucas of Simpson, Galliti, Tel. No. 1-650-615-4866 appearing for
the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Goodfellow Top Grade Construction, LLC's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Triable issues of material fact exist as to whether Goodfellow's failures to comply with Labor Code 226 were "knowing and intentional" rather than "isolated and unintentional." (See Kao v. Holiday (2017) 12 Cal.App.4th 947, 961-62.) This is particularly so as the failures apparently occurred some 1,500 times over a period of many months. (PMF 5-8.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/ru
